data.average_yield_kg_ha: CT: 3937.2 kg/ha, CA-sole maize: 4335.8 kg/ha, CA maize–cowpea rotation: 4388.3 kg/ha (2010–2030 mean)
data.adaptation_notes: Moderately tolerant to low nitrogen conditions; preferred by farmers for white, semi-flint grain with good poundability and storage qualities. CA practices (no-till, mulch) improved yield stability and performance, especially in low rainfall years. The fertilizer at planting and 46 kg ha1 N in the form of urea 21 days after planting. values of weather parameters, crop management and soil properties during the 2007–2008 cropping season of the experiment. This variety is tolerant to a range of maize diseases including maize streak virus (MSV), gray leaf spot (GLS) and Turcicum leaf blight; is moderately tolerant to low nitrogen conditions and is liked by farmers because it is white and semi-ﬂint, which improves the poundability and storage qualities of the maize. Genetic coefﬁcients were estimated by using observed silking and maturity dates and grain yield of the maize variety SC627 for all treatments during the growing season with remaining seasons 2008–2009 to 2011–2012 used for subsequent model evaluation (Sarkar and Kar, 2006; Saseendran et al., 2013).

location_contexts.management_context: Conventional tillage (CT), Conservation agriculture (CA) with no-till and mulch, CA maize–cowpea rotation
location_contexts.evidence: A medium maturing maize hybrid SC627 was used at the study site... The objective of this experiment, established in the 2007/2008 season, was to evaluate and monitor medium to longer term effects of CA practices... The trial had seven treatments... Chitedze is located on the mid-altitude agro-ecological zone of Malawi.

coefficients.notes: Extracted from a paper-reported DSSAT coefficient table.

data.average_yield_kg_ha: CT: 3937.2 kg/ha; CA-sole maize: 4335.8 kg/ha; CA maize–cowpea rotation: 4388.3 kg/ha (2010–2030 projections, Chitedze)
data.adaptation_notes: Moderately tolerant to low nitrogen conditions; white, semi-flint grain type preferred by farmers for poundability and storage; CA practices (no-till, mulch) stabilize yields under variable rainfall and outperform CT in dry years. Some of the crop management data required to simulate DSSAT included crop, cultivar, planting date, row and plant spacing, fertilizer levels, tillage practices and organic amendments (Jones et al., 2003). values of weather parameters, crop management and soil properties during the 2007–2008 cropping season of the experiment. Yields of velvet beans and pigeonpea were very low due to competition for light, moisture stress and pest attacks, respectively. Genetic coefﬁcients were estimated by using observed silking and maturity dates and grain yield of the maize variety SC627 for all treatments during the growing season with remaining seasons 2008–2009 to 2011–2012 used for subsequent model evaluation (Sarkar and Kar, 2006; Saseendran et al., 2013).

location_contexts.management_context: Conventional tillage (CT), Conservation agriculture (CA) sole maize, CA maize–cowpea rotation
location_contexts.evidence: A medium maturing maize hybrid SC627 was used at the study site. This variety is tolerant to a range of maize diseases... The seven genetic coefficients of cultivar SC627 of maize, which were calculated in the present study, are presented in Table 7. The CERES-Maize model was evaluated by comparing simulated and observed yield data for 2008–2009, 2009–2010, 2010–2011 and 2011–2012 growing seasons for all the three treatments used in DSSAT.

coefficients.notes: Extracted from a paper-reported DSSAT coefficient table.

Yields of velvet beans and pigeonpea were very low due to competition for light, moisture stress and pest attacks, respectively. Genetic coefﬁcients were estimated by using observed silking and maturity dates and grain yield of the maize variety SC627 for all treatments during the growing season with remaining seasons 2008–2009 to 2011–2012 used for subsequent model evaluation (Sarkar and Kar, 2006; Saseendran et al., 2013).
